concepts.title: Properties of inverse trigonometric functions part 2
concepts.conceptBody:
Properties of inverse trigonometric functions

(i) $\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\left( -\text{x} \right)=-\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\text{x }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }$ Provided that $-1\le \text{x}\le 1$

Let $\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\left( -x \right)=\theta $

or $-\text{x}=\text{sin}\theta \Rightarrow \text{x}=-\text{sin}\theta $ or $\text{x}=\text{sin}\left( -\theta \right)$

or $-\theta =\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}x\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }$ or $\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\theta =-\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}x$

$\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\left( -\text{x} \right)=-\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\text{x}$

(ii) $\text{co}{{\text{s}}^{-1}}\left( -\text{x} \right)=\pi -\text{co}{{\text{s}}^{-1}}\text{x }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }$ Provided that $-1\le \text{x}\le 1$

(iii) $\text{tai}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\left( -\text{x} \right)=-\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\text{x }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }$ Provided that $-\infty <\text{x}<\infty $

(iv) $\text{co}{{\text{t}}^{-1}}\left( -\text{x} \right)=\pi -\text{co}{{\text{t}}^{-1}}\text{x }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }$ Provided that $-\infty <\text{x}<\infty $

(v) $\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{-1}}\left( -\text{x} \right)=\pi -\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{-1}}\text{x }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }$ Provided that $-\infty <\text{x}\le 1$ or $1\le \text{x}<\infty $

(vi) $\text{cose}{{\text{c}}^{-1}}\left( -\text{x} \right)-\text{cose}{{\text{c}}^{-1}}\text{x }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }$ Provided that $-\infty <\text{x}\le -1$ or $1\le \text{x}<\infty $

concepts.title: Conversion Properties
concepts.conceptBody:
Conversion Property 

(i) $\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}=\text{cose}{{\text{c}}^{-1}}\left( \frac{1}{x} \right);-1\le x\le 1$ and $\text{cose}{{\text{c}}^{-1}}x=\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\left( \frac{1}{x} \right),x\in R-\left( -1,1 \right)$ 

Let $\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\text{x}=\text{y }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\Rightarrow \text{x}=\text{siny}\Rightarrow \text{cosecy}=\frac{1}{\text{x}}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\Rightarrow \text{y}=\text{cose}{{\text{c}}^{-1}}\frac{1}{\text{x}}$

$\Rightarrow \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ si}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}x=\text{cose}{{\text{c}}^{-1}}\left( \frac{1}{x} \right)$

(ii) $\text{co}{{\text{s}}^{-1}}x=\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{-1}}\left( \frac{1}{x} \right)\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }-1\le x\le 1$ and $\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{-1}}x=\text{co}{{\text{s}}^{-1}}\left( \frac{1}{x} \right),x\in R-\left( 1,1 \right)$ 

(iii) $\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}x=\text{co}{{\text{t}}^{-1}}\left( \frac{1}{x} \right)\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }x\in R$ and $\text{co}{{\text{t}}^{-1}}x=\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\left( \frac{1}{x} \right),x>0=\pi +\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\left( \frac{1}{x} \right)x<0$ 

$=\pi +\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\left( \frac{1}{x} \right)x<0$
